How many commits are there in total?:
```python
git_project = graph_data["git"]
total_commits = len(git_project.git_commit_registry.all)
print(f"There are {total_commits} commits in total.")
```
Who is the most active author?:
```python
from collections import Counter

git_project = graph_data["git"]
author_commit_count = Counter()

for commit in git_project.git_commit_registry.all:
    author_commit_count[commit.author] += 1

most_active_author = author_commit_count.most_common(1)

if most_active_author:
    author, count = most_active_author[0]
    print(f"The most active author is {author.git_id} with {count} commits.")
else:
    print("No authors found.")
```
How many issues are in the project?:
```python
jira_project = graph_data["jira"]
num_issues = len(jira_project.issue_registry.all)

print(f"There are {num_issues} issues in the project.")
```
Show me all pull requests:
```python
github_project = graph_data["github"]

print("List of all pull requests:")
for pull_request in github_project.pull_request_registry.all:
    print(f"Pull Request #{pull_request.number}: {pull_request.title}")
```
